content.problemStatement:
Let BBB be an array of size M (M≥2)M \ (M \ge 2)M (M≥2). We define f(B)f(B)f(B) as ∑i=1M−1(Bi+Bi+1)\displaystyle{\sum_{i = 1}^{M - 1} (B_i + B_{i + 1})}i=1∑M−1​(Bi​+Bi+1​).

You are given an array AAA of size NNN, and an integer KKK.

Find the maximum value of f(C)f(C)f(C) where CCC denotes a KKK-sized subsequence of array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and KKK - the size of the array AAA and the size of the required subsequence.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the maximum value of f(C)f(C)f(C) where CCC denotes a KKK-sized subsequence of array AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : The subsequence C=[1,15,7]C = [1, 15, 7]C=[1,15,7] has the maximum value, which is (1+15)+(15+7)=38(1 + 15) + (15 + 7) = 38(1+15)+(15+7)=38. It can be proven that all other subsequences have a smaller value.
Test case 333 : Since K=NK = NK=N, we must choose the whole array as CCC, i.e. C=[1,2,3,4,5,6]C = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]C=[1,2,3,4,5,6] and f(C)=35f(C) = 35f(C)=35.
